channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: What AIFS' family formation research could mean for early childhood education and care
item.description: Australia's latest research into young people's aspirations for parenthood may not have been written with early childhood education and care (ECEC) in mind, but it offers valuable insights into the environment in which future families are making decisions.
item.content:encoded: Australia's latest research into young people's aspirations for parenthood may not have been written with early childhood education and care (ECEC) in mind, but it offers valuable insights into the environment in which future families are making decisions.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Women educators share stories of leadership, resilience and connection at inaugural She Leads event
item.description: More than 75 women educators from across the Glenfield Principals Network have come together to celebrate leadership, resilience and professional growth at the inaugural She Leads: Stories of Success and Strength event, creating a new platform to support women in educational leadership across New South Wales.
item.content:encoded: More than 75 women educators from across the Glenfield Principals Network have come together to celebrate leadership, resilience and professional growth at the inaugural She Leads: Stories of Success and Strength event, creating a new platform to support women in educational leadership across New South Wales.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Services reminded to rehearse and document emergency procedures every three months
item.description: Early childhood education and care services are being encouraged to use a structured observer checklist to evaluate their emergency and evacuation rehearsals and identify opportunities to strengthen their response procedures.
item.content:encoded: Early childhood education and care services are being encouraged to use a structured observer checklist to evaluate their emergency and evacuation rehearsals and identify opportunities to strengthen their response procedures.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Full-time kindergarten pilot expands to five more WA schools in 2027 - The Sector
item.description: Full-time kindergarten pilot expands to five more WA schools in 2027 - The Sector
item.content:encoded: Full-time kindergarten pilot expands to five more WA schools in 2027 - The Sector
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Full-time kindergarten pilot expands to five more WA schools in 2027
item.description: Western Australia’s free, optional full-time kindergarten pilot will expand to five additional public schools in 2027, taking the program to 10 sites across metropolitan, regional and remote communities.
item.content:encoded: Western Australia’s free, optional full-time kindergarten pilot will expand to five additional public schools in 2027, taking the program to 10 sites across metropolitan, regional and remote communities.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Fraser Foundation invests $160,000 in Australia’s next generation of early childhood teachers
item.description: The Fraser Foundation has committed more than $160,000 to aspiring early childhood teachers across five states and territories in its latest scholarship round, approving funding for more than 80 per cent of applicants.
item.content:encoded: The Fraser Foundation has committed more than $160,000 to aspiring early childhood teachers across five states and territories in its latest scholarship round, approving funding for more than 80 per cent of applicants.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Not so terrible, after all – 2-year-olds just want to be independent
item.description: Caring for a toddler can be challenging and requires patience. Understanding the psychology behind a toddler’s actions can help weather some of the rockier days.
item.content:encoded: Caring for a toddler can be challenging and requires patience. Understanding the psychology behind a toddler’s actions can help weather some of the rockier days.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: When growth outpaces your systems: how Aspire Early Education found confidence to change
item.description: For a growing early childhood education and care (ECEC) provider, growth can outpace the systems meant to support it. More centres mean more families to welcome, more compliance to manage, and more decisions to make quickly and with confidence.
item.content:encoded: For a growing early childhood education and care (ECEC) provider, growth can outpace the systems meant to support it. More centres mean more families to welcome, more compliance to manage, and more decisions to make quickly and with confidence.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Moving beyond suggestion boxes: children and young people’s voice in OSHC routines and environments
item.description: A suggestion box can be a useful tool.
item.content:encoded: A suggestion box can be a useful tool.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Everyone experiences loss and grief – but that doesn’t make it any easier to talk with kids about it
item.description: Parents and educators sometimes avoid having conversations about grief with children because they don’t know what to say. Here are some tips for how to approach it.
item.content:encoded: Parents and educators sometimes avoid having conversations about grief with children because they don’t know what to say. Here are some tips for how to approach it.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: The Breadwinner: Comedy doesn’t need flawless dads, but we do need competent ones
item.description: Comedic portrayals of incompetent dads can distort public understanding of fathers’ essential and real-life contributions to children’s development.
item.content:encoded: Comedic portrayals of incompetent dads can distort public understanding of fathers’ essential and real-life contributions to children’s development.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Childcare workers have been guaranteed a pay bump. What’s the fine print?
item.description: The federal government has set aside $3.6 billion over the next two years for early educator pay. But what happens after that is not yet clear.
item.content:encoded: The federal government has set aside $3.6 billion over the next two years for early educator pay. But what happens after that is not yet clear.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Toy Story 5 pits traditional toys vs a tablet. In real life, families can combine the two
item.description: The latest instalment of the film franchise addresses a dilemma many parents face: what happens to playtime when children become obsessed with screens?
